After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text: / - What is the difference between the application and admission period? - You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period according to Universities Act 2002)](#c955669). - You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (=registration period for the continuation of studies according to Universities Act 2002)](#c955681). This is only possible, if you applied for admission within the application period and fulfil all admission requirements. - What is the Decree on Groups of Persons? Persons belonging to a group of persons according to the Decree on Groups of Persons (“PersGV”) may apply for admission to degree programmes within the application period for EU/EEA/CH citizens. They can be treated as equal to EU/EEA citizens regarding the tuition fee regulations. [Groups of persons and the required proofs](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/) - Is there a grace period, in case I miss the application period? No, there is no grace period. You have to submit your application for admission within the application period that applies to you in any case. - The admission period is still ongoing, however the application period is already over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester

retrieved.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: General information about the resumption of studies
retrieved.text:
Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 31 October 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

Our tip: Submit your application as early as possible!

As part of alternative extensions, you do not take a prescribed EC, but instead look for other courses or examinations that can be assigned to your own programme. Which achievements can be recognised? Courses and examinations from... 1. the range offered by your own degree programme at the University of Vienna, provided that the registration requirements are met and places are available; 2. other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, provided that the registration requirements are met and places are available; 3. other degree programmes at other recognised domestic post-secondary educational institutions; 4. recognised foreign post-secondary educational institutions within the framework of mobility programmes with advance notification or via the [Circle U. Open Campus.](https://www.circle-u.eu/open-campus) Other options: - Courses, examinations, and certificates at post-secondary educational institutions that focus on the certification of foreign language skills; - Austrian National Union of Students activity according to HSG, whereby 1 semester hour

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: and exams via u:find resp. the record of examinations in u:space. In order to be able to attend courses with continuous assessment or exams, you have to register in due time. You can find the registration periods within the respective courses in u:find. In order to register successfully, you have to fulfil the requirements according to the curriculum of your degree programme. This is being evaluated in the registration process. It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. You cannot be registered after the registration/late registration period has ended. If you have registered correctly, you are entitled to attend the course/exam. retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/anmeldeuebersicht) - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Registration deadline for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30189&cHash=de0ff6f2f9646d32b8000b74fd539a40) - [Registration for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30193&cHash=7fbe81eeddcfae3bd66265dbbd660f93) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Course with non-continuous assessment (npi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31201&cHash=60e218c8de231c88ec12793220a06022) - [Allocation of places in courses and

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. For example: You start your bachelor's programme in the winter semester of 2022/2023. You neither interrupt your studies nor take a leave of absence. You must obtain the minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2024 the latest. The deadline is on 31 October 2024. If you take a leave of absence, the semesters during which you are on leave do not count. After the end of your leave of absence, the semesters are counted again until you have studied for four semesters. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

reranked.title: Deadlines and dates
reranked.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending courses is - possible until the end of the [admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) if you apply for a leave of absence during the admission period and were already admitted in the previous semester. - possible until the date of application if you apply for leave of absence after the end of the admission period. You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester

As part of alternative extensions, you do not take a prescribed EC, but instead look for other courses or examinations that can be assigned to your own programme. Which achievements can be recognised? Courses and examinations from... 1. the range offered by your own degree programme at the University of Vienna, provided that the registration requirements are met and places are available; 2. other degree programmes at the University of Vienna, provided that the registration requirements are met and places are available; 3. other degree programmes at other recognised domestic post-secondary educational institutions; 4. recognised foreign post-secondary educational institutions within the framework of mobility programmes with advance notification or via the [Circle U. Open Campus.](https://www.circle-u.eu/open-campus) Other options: - Courses, examinations, and certificates at post-secondary educational institutions that focus on the certification of foreign language skills; - Austrian National Union of Students activity according to HSG, whereby 1 semester hour

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: and exams via u:find resp. the record of examinations in u:space. In order to be able to attend courses with continuous assessment or exams, you have to register in due time. You can find the registration periods within the respective courses in u:find. In order to register successfully, you have to fulfil the requirements according to the curriculum of your degree programme. This is being evaluated in the registration process. It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. You cannot be registered after the registration/late registration period has ended. If you have registered correctly, you are entitled to attend the course/exam. For courses with non-continuous assessment (e.g. lectures) the following status values may appear: - Registered (Angemeldet): You have registered for the course. - Deregistered (Abgemeldet): You have successfully deregistered from the course. For courses with continuous assessment (e.g. seminars,

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

reranked.title: ABC of terminology

If you state an appropriate reason, you can apply for the deletion of your examination attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within 14 days after your exam has been marked with an “X”. You will receive an official notice informing you about whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Discontinuing an exam/course with continuous assessment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30167&cHash=040ff36417e980db488e9aba95efa9ef)

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. Related Links: - [Registration overview in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/anmeldeuebersicht) - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Registration deadline for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30189&cHash=de0ff6f2f9646d32b8000b74fd539a40) - [Registration for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30193&cHash=7fbe81eeddcfae3bd66265dbbd660f93) - [Registration system for courses and exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30195&cHash=ec6c0ec1dfc7c02a395a68ae4b1740d9) - [Course with non-continuous assessment (npi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31201&cHash=60e218c8de231c88ec12793220a06022) - [Allocation of places in courses and

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. For example: You start your bachelor's programme in the winter semester of 2022/2023. You neither interrupt your studies nor take a leave of absence. You must obtain the minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2024 the latest. The deadline is on 31 October 2024. If you take a leave of absence, the semesters during which you are on leave do not count. After the end of your leave of absence, the semesters are counted again until you have studied for four semesters. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
reranked.text:
Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

selected.title: Application and admission periods
selected.text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

selected.title: Deadlines and dates
selected.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

selected.title: Deadlines and dates
selected.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? [2] Leave of absence — FAQ
/ - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

[3] Application and admission periods — FAQ
over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

[4] Deadlines and dates
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[6] Deadlines and dates
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# Deadlines and dates

Are you interested in a bachelor's/diploma programme with an entrance exam/aptitude test procedure? On this page, you will find all the information you need about our application and admission deadlines and test dates for the academic year 2026/27.

- ### Application period

  The application period is the time frame during which you can apply to study at the University of Vienna by submitting your application for admission.

  During the application period, you can apply for the following: first-time admission, change of programme, admission to an additional programme, and resumption of studies.

  Our tip: Apply right at the beginning of the application period! This will allow you to complete the admission process quickly after the admission period begins.
- ### Admission period

  During the admission period, you complete all the necessary steps in the admission process and are officially admitted.

  Please note: This is only possible if you have applied within the application period and submitted an application for admission to your desired degree programme. You must also meet all admission requirements.

  Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as early as possible. That way, you will get off to a good start in your studies!

You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment?
